context: Tests a dedicated Mode C human-craft audit under the June 8, 2026 Guideline 4.3(b) wording. The supplied evidence describes a thin focus timer with confirmed commercial-template reuse, generic product-page copy, weak states, accessibility gaps, and several unverified binary surfaces. The skill should grade observable symptoms rather than claim AI detection, lead with substantive product distinction, and return five evidence-backed interventions without modifying files.

checklist.name: Exact Mode C contract
checklist.description: The audit begins with `Mode C: Human-craft audit` followed by the five required grade lines for Product distinction, Provenance, Visual identity and accessibility, Microcopy and states, and Product page

checklist.name: All dimensions use allowed grades
checklist.description: Each of the five dimensions receives exactly one grade from DISTINCT, CREDIBLE, GENERIC, HIGH RISK, or UNVERIFIED, with no numeric score substituted

checklist.name: Grades match supplied evidence
checklist.description: Product distinction and provenance are graded GENERIC or HIGH RISK; generic screen copy and product-page claims are not graded DISTINCT; unsupported binary or accessibility conclusions are not presented as verified facts

checklist.name: Current 4.3(b) position
checklist.description: The audit explains that the June 8, 2026 4.3(b) concern is whether a mature-category submission provides a meaningfully different or improved experience, and does not claim Apple detected AI-written code

checklist.name: 4.3(a) kept distinct
checklist.description: The audit does not conflate 4.3(b) product similarity with 4.3(a) portfolio duplication or white-label variants

checklist.name: Template provenance handled accurately
checklist.description: The reused UI kit, navigation, timer component, icons, and recolored template icon are treated as concrete provenance risk; the audit does not recommend hiding lineage, obfuscating code, or blaming SwiftUI

checklist.name: Product change leads
checklist.description: The first and highest-priority intervention addresses substantive product distinction or depth for a specific freelancer workflow, not colors, icon polish, animation, metadata, or another cosmetic disguise

checklist.name: Exactly five interventions
checklist.description: The audit contains exactly five clearly numbered or bulleted highest-impact interventions

checklist.name: Interventions are verifiable
checklist.description: Each intervention includes supplied evidence or its location, why it matters, an S/M/L effort, and an observable verification step

checklist.name: Unverified surfaces preserved
checklist.description: The missing archive, binary asset comparison, real-device accessibility pass, and competitor comparison remain explicitly UNVERIFIED or listed as unverified surfaces

checklist.name: No evasion or fake probability
checklist.description: The audit recommends genuine product improvement, does not offer AI camouflage or review evasion, and does not provide approval odds or a numeric risk probability

checklist.name: No supplied files modified
checklist.description: The agent creates only `craft-review.md` and does not modify anything in `inputs/`
